description_long: This is a cylindrical stone water well with a polygonal base, constructed from large, unevenly shaped stone blocks in shades of greenish-gray. The well features a wooden roof supported by two vertical posts, with a slightly curved, dark brown wooden plank forming the roof itself. A horizontal wooden beam spans the posts, supporting a hand crank mechanism with a handle and a visible rope or chain for drawing water. The interior of the well is open, revealing a hollow space that would typically contain water. The overall design is rustic and utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or modern enhancements.
description: A rustic stone water well with a wooden roof, hand crank, and open interior, built from large greenish-gray stone blocks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the hand crank, rope, and open well mouth beneath a wooden roof supported by two posts.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the back of the wooden roof and posts, with the stone well wall and no visible crank mechanism.
description_view_2: Opposite front view with the crank handle on the right, showing the open well and wooden roof structure.
description_view_3: Rear angled view highlighting the back of the roof and posts, with the stone wall and no visible interactive parts.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A hand crank mechanism with a rope is present, mounted horizontally between two wooden posts.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a high stone wall forming the main body, approximately waist height.
